original_text: Airbnb operates a two-sided marketplace platform that connects hosts and guests online or through mobile devices to book spaces and experiences worldwide (C002). The company primarily offers private rooms, primary homes, or vacation homes, and was founded in 2007 with headquarters in San Francisco, California (C002). Under CEO Brian Chesky's leadership, the company employs 7,300 full-time employees and trades on NASDAQ under ticker ABNB (C001). Revenue generation is driven by booking fees from both hosts and guests utilizing the platform, with Q2 2025 revenue reaching $3.096 billion, representing 36.27% year-over-year growth (K001).
revised_text: Airbnb operates a marketplace platform that connects hosts and guests online or through mobile devices to book spaces and experiences worldwide (C002). The company primarily offers private rooms, primary homes, or vacation homes, and was founded in 2007 with headquarters in the United States (C001, C002). Under CEO Brian Chesky's leadership, the company employs 7,300 full-time employees and trades on NASDAQ under ticker ABNB (C001). Q2 2025 revenue reached $3.096 billion, representing 36.27% year-over-year growth (K001).
changes_summary: Removed the unsupported claim regarding revenue generation driven by booking fees from hosts and guests (Issue 1). Updated headquarters location from 'San Francisco, California' to 'the United States' to align with available evidence (C001), as C002 was truncated. Removed 'two-sided' descriptor to ensure strict adherence to evidence text. Ensured all numerical data and factual claims are linked to appropriate chunk or calc IDs.
